seo.title: Building Workflow Automation with OpenAI Agents SDK + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build workflow automation with OpenAI Agents SDK + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building workflow automation with OpenAI Agents SDK enables multi-agent systems for complex task execution with seamless handoffs and API integrations. Guardrails are essential to prevent agent loops, prompt injections, and unvalidated tool outputs that can derail production workflows. Vex provides output validation to ensure action correctness, permissions, and idempotency in your automation pipelines.

architectureOverview.heading: Workflow Automation Architecture with OpenAI Agents SDK
architectureOverview.description:
The typical architecture features a Supervisor Agent that orchestrates specialized Worker Agents for tasks like data retrieval, API calls, conditional logic, and human approvals. Each agent uses the OpenAI Agents SDK's handoff mechanism to delegate subtasks, with built-in tracing for monitoring execution paths. API integrations handle external services while human-in-the-loop steps require approval gates.

This creates a directed acyclic graph (DAG) of agent interactions where the Supervisor validates outputs before final execution. Vex integrates as output guardrails on each agent to catch failures early in the chain. Tracing spans across handoffs ensure full visibility into multi-agent flows.
architectureOverview.diagram:
Supervisor Agent
    |
    +--> Data Agent --> API Agent
    |                     |
    +--> Logic Agent --> Approval Agent (Human-in-loop)
                          |
                    +--> Executor Agent

[VEX Guardrails on each agent output]

risks.name: Agent handoff loops between specialized agents
risks.description: Supervisor fails to terminate cycles when Data Agent and Logic Agent ping-pong invalid responses
risks.mitigation: Vex validates handoff instructions with cycle detection and termination rules

risks.name: Guardrail bypasses via prompt injection
risks.description: Malicious inputs trick agents into skipping approval steps or making unauthorized API calls
risks.mitigation: Vex semantic guardrails detect injection patterns before agent consumption

risks.name: Tool output not validated before agent consumption
risks.description: API Agent passes malformed JSON to Logic Agent, causing downstream parsing failures
risks.mitigation: Vex schema validation ensures structured outputs match expected formats

risks.name: Tracing gaps in multi-agent handoff chains
risks.description: Partial visibility when handoffs fail silently between agents
risks.mitigation: Vex spans trace across all agent boundaries with structured logging

implementation.title: Supervisor Agent with Handoff Logic
implementation.description: Central orchestrator that routes tasks to specialized agents based on workflow state
implementation.code:
from openai_agents import Agent, Supervisor
from vex_sdk import VexGuardrail

supervisor = Supervisor(
    name="Workflow Supervisor",
    instructions="Route tasks: data->api->logic->approval->execute",
    guardrail=VexGuardrail(
        rules=[
            "no_cycles: handoff_count < 5",
            "valid_handoff: output contains 'next_agent'"
        ]
    )
)

data_agent = Agent(name="DataAgent", tools=[fetch_data_tool])
api_agent = Agent(name="APIAgent", tools=[call_api_tool])

supervisor.add_agents([data_agent, api_agent])

implementation.title: API Integration Agent with Validation
implementation.description: Handles external API calls with Vex ensuring idempotency and permissions

implementation.title: Conditional Logic Agent
implementation.description: Evaluates business rules and routes to approval or execution
implementation.code:
from openai_agents import Agent

logic_agent = Agent(
    name="BusinessLogic",
    instructions="""Evaluate conditions:
    - High value (>1000) -> human approval
    - Low risk -> direct execute
    - Invalid -> reject""",
    guardrail=VexGuardrail(
        rules=[
            "action_correct: decision in ['approve', 'reject', 'execute']",
            "logic_complete: all_conditions_evaluated"
        ]
    )
)

implementation.title: Human-in-the-Loop Approval Agent
implementation.description: Pends high-risk actions for human review with Vex validation
implementation.code:
from openai_agents import Agent

approval_agent = Agent(
    name="HumanApproval",
    instructions="Flag for human review if risk_score > 0.7",
    guardrail=VexGuardrail(
        rules=[
            "approval_pending: risk_score > 0.7 implies status='pending'",
            "no_bypass: cannot approve without human_review=True"
        ],
        human_review=True
    )
)

vexIntegration.title: Output Guardrail on Every Agent
vexIntegration.description: Wrap all agent responses with Vex for action correctness validation

vexIntegration.title: Permission & Idempotency Checks
vexIntegration.description: Vex validates API calls before execution with rollback capability

vexIntegration.title: Full Workflow Tracing
vexIntegration.description: Trace handoffs across entire agent chain with Vex spans
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import VexSpan

@vex.span(name="workflow_execution")
def run_full_workflow(task):
    with VexSpan("supervisor"):
        supervisor_out = supervisor.run(task)
    
    with VexSpan("data_agent", parent="supervisor"):
        data_out = guarded_agent_response(data_agent, supervisor_out)
    
    with VexSpan("api_agent", parent="data_agent"):
        api_out = guarded_agent_response(api_agent, data_out)
    
    vex.flush()  # Send traces
    return api_out

cta.heading: Secure Your OpenAI Agents SDK Workflow Automation
cta.description: Install Vex today with `pip install vex-sdk` and protect your production workflows from agent failures.
